description: Track E production model SSOT — which checkpoint is RIGHT NOW the user-facing production model. Single source of truth, updated only on promotion.

current.name: alm14b_r4_step_2000
current.promoted_at_note: Best guess from memory (project_session_20260412_night.md records 'R2 backup working: step_2000 + step_4000 uploaded'). Earlier checkpoint than later r4 steps (4000/6000/8000/10000) but task-specified as current production belief.

candidate.name: alm14b_r9_step_2000
candidate.nomination_rationale: Tool-use gate PASSED lenient judge at 0.933 avg_score / 0.867 completion (see shared/state/tool_use_gate_20260416.json) — exceeds v3.0 Agent destination criterion ≥0.80 by 0.133 margin. r10 regressed (0.700 completion) due to LoRA-on-LoRA over-training per shared/state/hire_sim_r10_regression_20260416.json — retire r10, promote r9 as canonical v2.0 RC + v3.0 Agent production candidate.

supersedes_r10.root_cause: LoRA-on-LoRA over-training on 300-record templated synthetic corpus; r10 final_loss=7.82 never dipped <7.7; violated feedback_no_resume_data_change by stacking on r9 adapter instead of fresh base

pending_eval.eval_loss: UNMEASURED — requires eval pipeline run on r9 step_2000
pending_eval.phi_holo: UNMEASURED — requires phi pipeline run on r9 step_2000

candidate.promotion_decision: CANDIDATE — pending eval metrics. Once eval_loss + phi_holo land, compare against current production (r4 step_2000: eval_loss=0.7104, phi_holo=2874.69). If r9 beats r4 on both OR ties-on-one-wins-on-tool-use, promote.

candidate_evaluation_2026_04_16.blocker: train_meta.json on R2 contains only training loss, NOT eval_loss or phi_holo. No separate eval result files exist in any checkpoint directory. Without eval_loss and phi_holo metrics, promotion criteria cannot be checked.
candidate_evaluation_2026_04_16.secondary_blocker: step_4000/6000/8000/10000 use lora_r=64 (different config from production step_2000 which uses lora_r=32). These are from a different training run, not simply later checkpoints of the same run. Adapter size: ~1.1GB vs production ~201MB.
candidate_evaluation_2026_04_16.note_on_current_production_metrics: The eval_loss=0.7104 recorded for step_2000 matches the training loss in its train_meta.json exactly (0.7103547677397728). The phi_holo=2874.69 metric has no on-R2 source — it was likely computed offline or from a separate evaluation pipeline.
observations: Training loss drops sharply from step_2000 (0.710) to step_4000 (0.019) — but these are different runs (r32 vs r64)
Training loss plateaus at step_8000 and step_10000 (both 0.01173) — possible convergence or overfitting
step_8000 and step_10000 have identical loss — step_10000 may offer no benefit over step_8000
No checkpoint can be promoted without running eval_loss and phi_holo evaluation offline
required_next_steps: Run eval pipeline on step_4000/6000/8000/10000 to get eval_loss and phi_holo
Run korean qualitative 5-turn pass on candidates
Only then can promotion criteria be evaluated

r2_verification.sha256_note: rclone ls only; per-file sha256 hashing deferred (next cycle). R2 ETag check also not yet run.

sibling_checkpoints_on_r2.note: Track E discovery — r4 has later checkpoints on R2 that are NOT current production. Evaluate in promotion cycles.
sibling_checkpoints_on_r2.config_divergence: step_500 through step_2000 use lora_r=32; step_4000 through step_10000 use lora_r=64 (different training run)
sibling_checkpoints_on_r2.metrics_available: training loss only (no eval_loss, no phi_holo on R2)
